Review of prior analysis:  Reentrancy   – Verified: the contract never performs an external call (`call`, `send`, `transfer`, `delegatecall`, etc.). No reentrancy pattern is possible.   – Prior verdict (“no reentrancy”) is correct.  Access-control   – Function `changeOwner(address _newOwner)` is `public` and lacks the `onlyowner` modifier, letting any account overwrite the `owner` variable.   – This is a classic improper-access-control issue for a sensitive ownership parameter.   – Prior verdict

Review of each class:  1. Reentrancy      • withdraw(uint256): performs `msg.sender.transfer(amount)` (an external call) before the internal balance is decreased (`balances[msg.sender] -= amount`).      • refund(): performs `msg.sender.transfer(balances[msg.sender])` and never touches the balance mapping at all.      Both functions therefore match the definition “external call before the contract updates its own state”.      Consequently, class reentrancy is genuinely present.  2. Access Control
